headline: South Korea's SK Hynix eyes US listing as soon as August, sources say
summary: STORY: SK Hynix reportedly plans to list its shares in the U.S. as soon as August.That's according to two sources familiar with the matter...The South Korean memory chipmaker wants to capitalise on strong appetite for AI-linked stocks and broaden its investor base.One source added the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission is likely to approve SK Hynix's American listing application during the week of June 22nd.SK Hynix said in March it had confidentially filed for a U.S. listing.A source said at the time the offering could raise as much as $14 billion.The company is the world's second-largest memory chipmaker and a key supplier to Nvidia.It's scored big in the AI boom due to its dominant position in high-bandwidth memory chips used in AI servers.Its share price has jumped 240% this year and its market value has topped $1 trillion since May.That makes it only the third Asian company after Taiwan's TSMC and Samsung Electronics to hit the milestone.An August debut would also add another top name to what is shaping up to be a busy second half for U.S. equity markets.
